title: June implementation-audit re-verification
manager: June's implementation audit found packaging and fidelity problems — pack shape, the interchange envelope, forced-transition erasure in codegen — but against a tree that has since moved, so its findings may now be memories rather than facts. This is the pass that reproduces or refutes each of the seven findings against both current main and the assembly line, fixes the cheap ones as 5.x patches, and feeds the rest into the bug burn-down. It is what lets 6.0 fix real problems instead of stale ones.
cs: reproduce/refute the seven findings on both branches (pack-shape via `npm pack --dry-run --json` assertions; advertised-verb/bin drift; interchange envelope + start-states loss; forced-transition erasure; config-schema drift; test type-import drift); append a dated verdict table to the era-0 brief Corrections; land the pack-shape test green.
